dialogue.utterance: All right, so can't get out of bed, confused.
We're thinking about all of our altered mental status.
I want to get a blood sugar.
I want to look for alcohol.
All right, [REDACTED], let's go ahead and grab the monitor.
Uh, let's bring the stretcher out to the door.
I want the stat pack and the monitor coming in with us.
Do you have the I can we get the tablet too?
Uh, can you, you're going to be on standby if we need to get extrication equipment, okay?
[REDACTED], you want to bring this in with me?
You want me to grab that?
But still come with us so I can tell you what to get, okay?
Tenant upstairs, 911 for a possible fall.
Uh, [REDACTED], um, I'm going to have you bring up the, uh, [REDACTED].
[REDACTED], I want you to come with me, bring this.
I got the LifePak over here, okay?
Let me show you where they're at.
Sir, do you know anything about what happened to this, uh, what happened to this woman?
I heard she had some like kind of altered mental status.
Like, you know, when a 911 call goes out, they just call me to come in and I just kind of open the door for you guys.
You know, when you were meeting her, like, have you ever met her?
Do you know like kind of how she is normally or?
Uh, not, you know, not that she's like fine, I guess.
I'm not really familiar, but um, yeah, uh, I think her neighbor's there.
I think they could probably tell you.
Her neighbor can tell us more?
What the hell took you so long?
We're, we're doing our best to get to you.
Can you start talking to him?
I'm an EMT with Charles County Mobile Rescue Squad.
Can she, any response to pain?
Uh, it's as you find it.
I have a strong carotid pulse.
Can you tell me what's going on right now?
I heard breath sounds over here.
And what's kind of respiratory rate?
We need to get her out of here.
I see chest rise and fall.
All right, I count a 24 respiratory rate.
All right, second [REDACTED] gets up here, we're just going to go.
You know, her air, she's breathing normally, her airway's fine.
Uh, she's as you find it, she's satting 100%.
Respiratory rate as you found it.
Um, anything else you want to ask for?
Uh, well, and then cardiac, I already checked her carotid pulse.
Yeah, why is she hooked up to the bed?
You can't open it that way.
I can't open it that way.
Is there anywhere around here that you know can open that?
Um, all right, let's go ahead and add, let's go ahead and add fire.
Uh, [REDACTED], we have bolt cutters in the truck downstairs.
Uh, [REDACTED] will not be grabbing the bolt cutters.
Uh, you can go ahead and have fire.
Um, you can try to use your scene to figure out how to undo the cuffs.
Try to use our scene to figure out how to undo the cuffs.
Um, we're requesting fire for um, bolt removal.
Anything about this for her airway?
She still has a 24 respiratory rate.
I'm not feeling like protecting her airway right now.
Do you want to go for at least an NPA though?
Do you want me to go for an NPA?
Okay, we can go for an NPA.
Uh, for this equipment, do you want me to actually open it?
Tell me if the plastic is like...
So I'm sizing the NPA by going from the nostril to the bottom of the earlobe.
Um, this one appears to fit, it's a 24.
Um, I'm then going to lube it up.
I'm going to go into her right nostril with the bevel to the septum.
We'll do head tilt, chin lift into the nostril.
What do we have in here?
She rented a room from me. I thought she was a decent person, but she turned out to be a whore.
Am I allowed to try to pry it or no?
Uh, try to like search for some way to do it.
She's a damn drug head drunk.
Um, do you know if she has any people coming by here recently, any...
Yeah, people coming by here all the damn time.
And do you know anything about why she might be in handcuffs?
She just was making a lot of noise and the police came over and checked and found her like this.
Let's get ready to move her.
Is pulse ox still satting at 100?
Pulse ox is still satting at 100.
Um, here's what we're going to do.
[REDACTED], you get on that side of the bed.
We're going to pull the bed forward this way.
Then we're going to send the [REDACTED] around over here and then we'll walk her out that way.
Let me know when you're ready.
So we're going to pull the bed towards [REDACTED].
Or we could direct carry her.
Try to do this without moving the bed.
It is bolted to the ground.
You want to direct carry her to the floor?
I don't want to do that.
And I got a respiratory rate of 20, so.
Oh, we can just shimmy her with the sheet that we have.
If we shimmy her over here to this side of the bed, then we can...
Um, but you can do, you can manipulate her on the bed as you feel.
So you think move her to the side of the bed, put the [REDACTED] on.
Put the, put the [REDACTED] on the bed.
We roll her to the side, get her on the [REDACTED].
And then we use the [REDACTED] to take her off, we get her down the stairs.
So we can't use a sheet?
Uh, you can't like pull with the sheet, but if you want to help you like roll her or something like that, you can do that.
Okay, well, so we have, we do have to move her to that corner though.
Or I guess, we might be able to roll her this way and put the [REDACTED] here.
Um, so like roll the [REDACTED], roll her this way, put the [REDACTED] right here?
So we put the [REDACTED] here and then we pull her this way and then we push her onto the [REDACTED] that way if we can reach over, or we can move her over there.
Which one do you want to do?
That was my first idea, I want to go with that.
You sure you need this one? Three?
I'm not on the sheet, sorry guys.
You want to get her roller and then put her on this?
Better move would have been to put it over there and then roll her towards us.
But it's okay, we already did it and we should just go.
We're going to roll her that way and then we'll put this under.
So [REDACTED], I'm going to have you, like I said, this is not ideal, we already made the wrong decision, but let's roll her this way and we'll put it under, okay?
So you're going to push this under, [REDACTED] and I are going to roll her.
Alright, you're going to put your hand here, I'm going to put my hand here.
Alright, let's grab her from this side.
Alright, let's get her strapped up.
Alright, [REDACTED], here's what's going to happen.
[REDACTED] and I are going to carry her.
You're going to put the stat pack on your back and then you're going to grab the, uh, you're going to grab the monitor.
I want to keep it with us while we're going.
And I got 20 again for respiratory rate.
Yep, that's what you find it.
Let's take the pulse ox off her just for this moment.
Mind just leaving it on here?
We can leave it on if you want.
And still satting at 100 on room air?
Actually, wait, I don't know if this one Metformin is.
I thought that's what Metformin was.
Any chance you could shove this under her shoulders just to like...
Just to get her head up.
Sir, we're doing everything we can.
Uh, no, it's going to bend her head.
Any chance you could get one more try and then I lift up and you go all the way under her shoulders?
Shove under there as far as you can.
Do we need to bring the pills with us?
No, that would be a good call.
Alright, [REDACTED], I want you to go out first and start heading down there.
Is this part of this scenario?
That is part of the scenario.
Okay, should I bring this too?
There's nothing we can do with that other than know that it's alcohol.
You're going to head downstairs and meet us at the, meet us at the stretcher, okay?
Uh, is the patient connected to the monitor?
Uh, that's not part of the scenario.
So again, you're going to lift her up, get her out the doorway, and then set her down once we get her outside the doorway?
Does that sound good to you?
Why are we setting her down?
Just to take a little break.
Sir, mind just leaning out of the way for us?
Sir, do you know if she took anything?
Did you see her take anything?
You want to take a break?
I don't know if she took some of that.
What's she like at, what's she like at baseline?
Or what is she like normally?
Carotid, if you feel the carotid, you know the carotid.
Do you want to go get that suction from the stretcher down there?
Try to keep her mouth open.
Let's go ahead and add ALS.
[REDACTED], Rescue 143 asking, uh, requesting ALS for an active seizure.
Uh, Rescue 140, I'm just dispatching paramedic [REDACTED].
Let's see if there's any secretions in there.
I don't see anything coming out of her, coming out of her mouth.
Is she still, she's still breathing as I see?
Whatever you see is what you have.
Whatever I see is what I have.
Um, let's get her hooked up to oxygen sats.
Uh, it is now down, going down to 94.

I'm thinking next stop, we try to do it at the top of the stairs.
Let's check her, you check her airway.
I'm going to check her carotid again.
Uh, it's as you feel it.
She's gone down to about 16 respiratory rate.
And where do you want to stop next time?
Uh, at the bottom of the stairs.
Let me know if I'm going too quick down the stairs.
Let's go ahead and stop on the ground.
Still don't see any secretions coming out.
Alright, next time we're, next place we're going to stop is right before the stairs, okay?
Alright, let's get ready to set her down.
Actually, let's come forward a little bit that way you can get out easier.
[REDACTED], do you want to bring that stuff downstairs?

Um, I'm thinking the way that she's positioned, you guys just go.
Um, and then where's, wait, hold on.

How about you go down the stairs and pull the stretcher down?
That way we can just go directly onto the stretcher.
That way we don't have to set her down again.
What are you going to keep monitoring?
What are you going to keep monitoring?
ABCs, which I'm doing right now.
You go at the head, I'm going to go at the feet.
Tell me if you need to take a break.
Alright, let's slow down just a little bit.
Still see her satting 100, is that correct?
Alright, I got a respiratory rate of 16, strong carotid.
I don't have a respiratory rate of 16.
I have a respiratory rate of 28.
What's the, what's, is her, I have a respiratory rate of 28.
Do you mind if I get her hooked up to end tidal?
Oh, that ain't going to fit.

Uh, paramedic [REDACTED] is there in the hospital.
They say it'll be around 20 minutes.

Let's check her ABCs one more time.
You check the airway, I'm going to check the carotid and the breathing, okay?
Actually, I'll check the carotid and the airway, you check the breathing.
I have a carotid, a strong carotid pulse.
We're going to transport emergently to [REDACTED].
109 over 60 is the correct blood pressure.

Uh, her vitals are as follows, state the vitals.

Should we wait until we get the blood sugar to, uh, to call?
Do you mind if I grab it to get it prepped for you please?

I have a 30-year-old female, uh, chief complaint is syncope.

Uh, your blood pressure is what it says right there.

Uh, skin feels as you find it.
Alright, this is Rescue 140 with a patient care report.

She has a blood, uh, she has a blood sugar of 70.

Vitals are as your last one.

Yep, I'm going to pull it up.
And you want to shove under?
The little thing we got under there.

Any contact information for anyone who knows her?

No, let's, let's get [REDACTED] out first last time.
